---
title: "Matrix A (Sosik)"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
library(reshape2)
library(ggplot2)
#equation 14 work - basically finding gamma (t)
Gamma_max <- 0.20
E_Light <- 1000 #proportion
E <- seq(0,1000, length.out = 300)
gamma_new <- Gamma_max * (1 - exp (-E / E_Light)) #eq 14

#This is for equation 13
v_min <- 2^-5
delta_v <- 0.125
m <- 57
i <- 1:m
v_i <- v_min * 2^ (( i - 1) * delta_v)

# equation 13 parameters
a <- 4.00
b <- 10.00
delta_max <- 0.06

#eq 13
delta_i <- (((a * v_i)^b) / (1 + (a * v_i)^b)) * delta_max

# now matrix A 
#for  square diagonal - growth - from i to i+1

gamma_t <- 0.10  # here I tried with gamma_new but my code didn't work,
                       # it said number of items to replace is not a 
                       # multiple of replacement length , so i tried with a fixed gamma at                              certain point 
A <- matrix(0, nrow = m, ncol = m)
for (i in 1: ( m-1 )){
  A[i+1, i] <- gamma_t * ( 1- delta_i [i])    #eq 7 
  
}

#  Division - from i to 1
j <- 1 + ( 1 / delta_v)
for (i in 2:(j-1)) {
  A[1,i] <- 2 * delta_i[i]
}
for (i in j:m) {
  A[i + 1 - j, i] <- 2 * delta_i[i]
}

# Stasis for i = 1

 A[1,1] <- ((1 - gamma_t) * ( 1 - delta_i[i]) ) + 2 * delta_i[i] 
 
for ( i in 2: ( m - 1)) {
 A[ i, i] <- (( 1 - gamma_t) * ( 1 - delta_i [i]) ) 
}
 A[ m,m] <- (1 - delta_i[i])

A

melted_A <- melt(A)
ggplot(data = melted_A, aes(x=Var1, y=Var2, fill=value)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) + scale_color_gradient(low = "#FFFFFF", high = "#E41A1C")

#samples
bin_centers <- c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
probabilities <- c(0.1, 0.2, 0.4, 0.2, 0.1) 
n <- 10000
simulated_sample <- sample(x = bin_centers, size = n, replace = TRUE, prob = probabilities)

```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{}
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).
